mechanism_narrative: Sequential increases in real GDP and consumer credit indicate expanding aggregate demand, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansionary demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations by raising the discount rate and signaling tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not correlate positively with the joint employment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its historical trend signals robust manufacturing output and supply chain health, which boosts industrial and materials sector earnings and lifts the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving business and consumer confidence in the near term, leading to increased construction activity and durable goods demand that supports equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower during high-inflation months compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and supporting higher equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not correlate positively with the joint expansion signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sub-4% unemployment rate indicates a tight labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, providing an earnings floor for equities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE stays below 4.0%.
